name: Cover Layout Intent
summary: A primitive layout region-role (#1680 taxonomy): composition-intent = a full-min-height region with a centered focal child between optional header/footer. Identity is the arrangement, not the CSS — FUI ships one block per role and the mechanism (flex-column + margin-block: auto on the focal child) is an impl seam. Project-less; per-project styling rides presentational variants + plateau assembler presets.

min-height.description: The region's minimum block-size (e.g. 100vh) — the role's essential knob. The cover is at least this tall, growing with content.

gap.description: Minimum spacing kept around the centered focal child, on a spacing scale.

centered.description: Which child is the focal, vertically-centered one (header and footer pin to the ends). Absent → the sole non-header/footer child.

description:
What This Is

A full-min-height region with an optional header and footer pinned to the ends and a focal child vertically centered in the remaining space — landing heroes, full-screen splash, empty states.

Composition-intent (the identity)

A primitive layout region-role (layoutTier: region-role), one of the per-role taxonomy ratified in #1680. Its identity is the composition-intent — a full-min-height region with a centered focal child between optional header/footer — impl-agnostic. FUI ships exactly one block for this role; the CSS mechanism (flex-column + margin-block: auto on the focal child) is an impl seam, never the role's identity (platform-decisions.md#layout-role-composition-intent).

Dimensions (UX-only)

  • min-height: The region's minimum block-size (e.g. 100vh) — the role's essential knob. The cover is at least this tall, growing with content.
  • gap: Minimum spacing kept around the centered focal child, on a spacing scale (none · xs · sm · md · lg · xl).
  • centered: Which child is the focal, vertically-centered one (header and footer pin to the ends). Absent → the sole non-header/footer child.

Annotation, not identity

The earns-role test is met: a vertically-centered full-height region is a distinct arrangement, not a variant of stack or center.
